text: The surgeon is dissecting the liver parenchyma using a harmonic scalpel, with the caudate lobe visibly detached from the inferior vena cava. During the transaction, a short hepatic vein tears at its origin from the cava, causing bleeding. Compression is applied to the left lobe of the liver using the third arm to manage the bleeding, while landmarks such as the vena cava, falciform ligament, and left hepatic vein are visible.

The surgeon completes the division of the left lobe of the liver, revealing a small tear on the vena cava at the bottom. The assistant suctions the area, and a selective suture is placed on the vena cava to control the bleeding. The vena cava is now fully exposed. The procedure involves a left hepatectomy extended to a portion of segment eight, with a Roux-en-Y reconstruction using a long jejunal loop for bile duct reconstruction.
